mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over three consecutive months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 3 consecutive months with cumulative loss > 100,000 jobs
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or post-shock labor adjustment
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following payroll declines are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail and food service sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above its 6-month trend, signaling robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth and equity valuations upward, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with RSAFS growth acceleration (Spearman rho < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contracts month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, indicating a supply-side shock combined with demand weakness that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1.3 million annualized units while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2% annualized, signaling demand-side weakness in both residential investment and overall economic activity that reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.3 million units AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST=1,450 thousand units, GDPC1=20,182.491 billion
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 2-month window, signaling monetary policy easing that reduces discount rates on future corporate cash flows and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or policy pivot
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% with t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 3.7% while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows steadily, indicating a tight labor market that sustains consumer income and spending power, supporting equity valuations through strong corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market or full employment regime
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of observations with UNRATE < 3.7%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 6-month trend while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable, signaling healthy supply-side expansion without inflationary pressure that supports corporate profit growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.3% MoM AND CPIAUCSL 3-month growth < 0.3%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
